{"content": "     1|# El Viajero — Deep Analysis & Cross-Repo Alignment Plan
     2|
     3|Date: [REDACTED_PHONE]
     4|Repo: github.com/Ai-Whisperers/elviajero-comercio
     5|Live: https://viajero.paragu-ai.com / https://el-viajero.paragu-ai.com
     6|
     7|---
     8|
     9|## 1. El Viajero Current State
    10|
    11|### Structure
    12|- Next.js 15, Tailwind 4, TypeScript
    13|- 107 component files (richest of all standalone sites)
    14|- 49 app pages + 20 API routes
    15|- SQLite database (`data/viajero.db`) + Supabase
    16|- Full admin panel (categorias, productos, pedidos, promos, usuarios, reportes, resenas, suscriptores, tema)
    17|- Full e-commerce: cart, checkout (Bancard, PagoPar, PayPal, Stripe), orders, shipping, coupons, BOGO, bundles
    18|
    19|### Content
    20|- `content/es.json` — 70KB, 3 locales (ES/EN/GN)
    21|- `content/tokens.json` — design tokens
    22|- `config/site.json` + `config/pages/*.json` — page config
    23|- `config/images.json` — image registry
    24|
    25|### Key Components (107 files)
    26|- Cart: cart-sidebar, cart-merger, cart-shipping, cart-multi-select, cart-related, cart-toast-listener
    27|- Ecom: add-to-cart, checkout-stepper, cod-option, coupon-input, delivery-calculator, frequently-bought, bulk-price, compare-checkbox, price-range
    28|- UX: hero-carousel, search-autocomplete, search-filters, recently-viewed, dark-mode-toggle, currency-switcher, language-switcher, exit-intent, cookie-consent, error-boundary, toast
    29|- Admin: 10 admin components (charts, image-upload, activity-log, etc.)
    30|- SEO: json-ld, article-json-ld, faq-json-ld, category-breadcrumb-jsonld, sitemap.ts, analytics.tsx, rss.xml
    31|- Core: header, footer, layout-client, bottom-nav, mobile-search, whatsapp-float
    32|- Auth: auth-wrapper, auth-context, login, register, mi-cuenta, recuperar
    33|
    34|### Maturity: HIGH — most feature-complete standalone site
    35|✅ Rich e-commerce flow
    36|✅ Multi-payment gateway
    37|✅ Full admin dashboard
    38|✅ SEO complete (sitemap, JSON-LD, RSS, OG, robots)
    39|✅ Analytics (GA4)
    40|✅ Auth system (custom pg JWT)
    41|✅ Cart persistence
    42|✅ Multi-currency
    43|✅ Multi-language (3 locales)
    44|✅ Dark mode
    45|✅ Cookie consent
    46|✅ Product comparison
    47|✅ Recently viewed
    48|
    49|---
    50|
    51|## 2. Cross-Repo Inventory — All Ai-Whisperers Client Sites
    52|
    53|### ACTIVE CLIENT WEBSITES (Standalone Docker Next.js)
    54|
    55|| Site | Repo | Local Path | Components | Pages | Content | Maturity | Status |
    56||------|------|-----------|-----------|-------|---------|----------|--------|
    57|| **El Viajero** | elviajero-comercio | /root/elviajero-comercio | 107 | 49 apps + 20 APIs | ES/EN/GN | HIGH | Live |
    58|| **Dayah LitWorks** | dayah-litworks | /root/dayah-litworks-deploy | 7 | 10 | ES/EN | MEDIUM | Live |
    59|| **Superspuma** | superspuma | /root/superspuma | 7 | 14 | ES only | MEDIUM | Live |
    60|| **Fun4Me** | fun4me | /root/fun4me-store | 0 | 30+ | ES | HIGH | Live |
    61|| **Clínica Duerksen** | clinica-duerksen | /root/clinica-duerksen | 28 | 15+ | DB-backed | HIGH | Live |
    62|| **Golden Visa** | golden-visa-advisory | /root/golden-visa-advisory | 0 | Scaffold | N/A | LOW | Live |
    63|| **Oz Montanía** | ozmontania-website | /root/ozmontania-website | 5 | 10 | ES/EN | MEDIUM | Live |
    64|| **3MD Creative** | 3md-website | /root/3md-website | 6 | 13 | ES | MEDIUM | Live |
    65|| **Depiflash** | depiflash | /root/depiflash | 7 | 6 | ES only | MEDIUM | Live |
    66|| **Stroopwafel Huis** | stroopwafel-huis | /root/stroopwafel-huis | 0 | Scaffold | N/A | LOW | Live |
    67|| **Granja Cabral** | granja-cabral | /root/granja-cabral | 0 | 5+ | ES | SCAFFOLD | Live |
    68|| **Bichos Gym** | bichos-gym | /root/bichos-gym | 7 | ~6 | ES | MEDIUM | Live |
    69|| **Cocodrilo Fitness** | cocodrilo-fitness | /root/cocodrilo-fitness | 7 | ~6 | ES | MEDIUM | Live |
    70|| **Mantra Spa** | mantra-spa | /root/mantra-spa | 7 | ~6 | ES | MEDIUM | Live |
    71|| **Luis de León** | luis-de-leon-concept | /root/luis-de-leon-concept | 7 | ~6 | ES | MEDIUM | Live |
    72|| **Magnolia Peluquería** | magnolia-peluqueria | /root/magnolia-peluqueria | 7 | ~6 | ES | MEDIUM | Live |
    73|
    74|### SCAFFOLD/PENDING SITES (Still in PAB builder, not standalone)
    75|
    76|| Site | PAB Sites Dir | PAB App Route | Needs Extraction |
    77||------|--------------|--------------|-----------------|
    78|| **Nexa Paraguay** | sites/nexa-paraguay | web/app/nexa-paraguay | YES |
    79|| **Nexa Propiedades** | sites/nexa-propiedades | NO | YES |
    80|| **Nudo** | sites/nudo | NO | YES |
    81|| **Stoicfinch** | sites/stoicfinch | NO | YES |
    82|| **De Abasto a Casa** | sites/de-abasto-a-casa | NO | YES |
    83|| **Bufete Méndez** | sites/bufete-mendez | NO | YES |
    84|| **Polki Squad** | sites/polki-squad | NO | YES |
    85|| **Alejandro Villamayor** | sites/alejandro-villamayor | NO | YES |
    86|| **Brahm the Raccoon** | sites/brahm-the-raccoon | NO | NO (has own repo) |
    87|
    88|### INTERNAL / NON-CLIENT REPOS
    89|
    90|| Repo | Purpose |
    91||------|---------|
    92|| **paragu-ai-builder** | Engine monorepo — the template source |
    93|| **paragu-ai** | Shared packages monorepo (8 packages) |
    94|| **client-kit** | Shared components (7 components) |
    95|| **template-nextjs-client** | Template scaffold for new sites |
    96|| **priorities** | Single source of truth |
    97|| **paragu-ai-leads** | Lead data |
    98|| **solstein / solstein-v2** | Internal prospecting |
    99|| **refugio-animal-paraguay** | Vet clinic platform |
   100|| **Vete** | Multi-tenant vet app |
   101|| **code-agent / code-agent-ui** | Internal agent |
   102|
   103|---
   104|
   105|## 3. Shared Architecture Patterns
   106|
   107|### Pattern A: Full E-commerce (El Viajero)
   108|```
   109|Standalone Next.js 15 + SQLite + Supabase + Auth + Admin
   110|Components in /components/ (107 files)
   111|Content in /content/es.json (70KB, 3 locales)
   112|Payments: Bancard, PagoPar, PayPal, Stripe
   113|Deploy: Docker Swarm + Traefik + CF
   114|```
   115|Used by: El Viajero only (most mature)
   116|
   117|### Pattern B: Static Showcase (Dayah / Superspuma / Depiflash / etc.)
   118|```
   119|Standalone Next.js + Tailwind 3/4 + content-driven
   120|Components from client-kit (7 shared: header, footer, whatsapp-float, cookie-consent, cta-banner, mobile-cta, process-section)
   121|Content in /content/es.json
   122|Deploy: Docker Swarm + Traefik + CF
   123|```
   124|Used by: Dayah, Superspuma, Depiflash, Bichos Gym, Cocodrilo, Mantra, Luis de León, Magnolia, Oz Montanía, 3MD
   125|**NOTE**: The 7 shared components are COPIED per-repo, not installed as packages (npm auth is broken)
   126|
   127|### Pattern C: Scaffold-only (Golden Visa / Stroopwafel)
   128|```
   129|Next.js scaffold — barely started
   130|No content structure, no real pages beyond layout
   131|```
   132|Used by: Golden Visa, Stroopwafel, Granja Cabral
   133|
   134|### Pattern D: Complex App (Clínica Duerksen / Fun4Me)
   135|```
   136|Next.js 15 + Supabase/PostgreSQL
   137|Custom auth, admin, booking, e-commerce
   138|Large component library (Duerksen: 28, Fun4Me: custom pages)
   139|```
   140|Used by: Clínica Duerksen, Fun4Me
   141|
   142|### Pattern E: PAB Monolith Tenant
   143|```
   144|paragu-ai-builder engine renders dynamically
   145|Content in sites/{tenant}/
   146|No standalone Docker — served through PAB
   147|```
   148|Used by: Nexa Paraguay, Nexa Propiedades, Nudo, Stoicfinch, De Abasto a Casa, Bufete Méndez, Polki Squad
   149|
   150|---
   151|
   152|## 4. Duplication Analysis
   153|
   154|### What's IDENTICAL across most repos (copied at scaffold time)
   155|- Dockerfile (7 sites have near-identical `node:20-alpine` 3-stage)
   156|- cookie-consent.tsx
   157|- cta-banner.tsx (in superspuma, depiflash, 3md)
   158|- footer.tsx
   159|- header.tsx
   160|- mobile-cta.tsx
   161|- process-section.tsx
   162|- whatsapp-float.tsx
   163|- content/tokens.json (same structure)
   164|- tsconfig.json (same structure)
   165|- docker-compose.yml (same Traefik labels)
   166|- .npmrc
   167|- postcss.config.mjs
   168|
   169|### What's UNIQUE to El Viajero (richest repo)
   170|- 107 components vs 7 for most other sites
   171|- Full admin panel (10 admin components)
   172|- e-commerce engine (cart, checkout, payments, shipping, coupons)
   173|- Auth system (login, register, profile, orders)
   174|- SEO suite (multiple JSON-LD types, sitemap, RSS)
   175|- Multi-language (3 locales)
   176|- Dark mode + currency switcher
   177|- Product comparison
   178|- Recently viewed + wishlist + back-in-stock
   179|- Exit intent + abandoned cart recovery
   180|- BOGO + bundles auto-promos
   181|
   182|### What's MISSING from most sites that El Viajero has
   183|1. **Admin panel** — only El Viajero + Duerksen have one
   184|2. **E-commerce cart** — only El Viajero + Fun4Me have real carts
   185|3. **Multi-payment** — only El Viajero (4 gateways)
   186|4. **GA4 analytics** — El Viajero ✅, Dayah broken, others missing
   187|5. **Cookie consent** — present in 7 sites, missing in Fun4Me, Oz, Golden Visa, Stroopwafel, Granja Cabral
   188|6. **JSON-LD** — only El Viajero + Dayah + 3MD have it
   189|7. **Dark mode** — only El Viajero
   190|8. **Search** — only El Viajero + Fun4Me
   191|9. **RSS feed** — only El Viajero + template
   192|10. **Recent products** — only El Viajero + template
   193|
   194|---
   195|
   196|## 5. Shared Infrastructure
   197|
   198|### paragu-ai monorepo (packages, never installed due to npm auth)
   199|Packages defined but NEVER actually installed in any client:
   200|- `@paragu-ai/theme` — design tokens
   201|- `@paragu-ai/content` — content schema
   202|- `@paragu-ai/ui` — UI components
   203|- `@paragu-ai/seo` — SEO components
   204|- `@paragu-ai/whatsapp` — WhatsApp utilities
   205|- `@paragu-ai/i18n` — i18n
   206|- `@paragu-ai/analytics` — GA4 wrapper
   207|- `@paragu-ai/commerce` — cart/checkout
   208|- `@paragu-ai/engine` — section composition
   209|- `@paragu-ai/tokens` — color tokens
   210|
   211|**Reality**: ALL packages are defined but NONE are published/installed. The client-kit `src/` files are the closest to working shared code, but even those are copied per-repo.
   212|
   213|### client-kit repo
   214|7 components in `src/components/`:
   215|- cookie-consent.tsx
   216|- cta-banner.tsx
   217|- footer.tsx
   218|- header.tsx
   219|- mobile-cta.tsx
   220|- process-section.tsx
   221|- whatsapp-float.tsx
   222|- index.ts (barrel export)
   223|
   224|Built with tsup, but never actually depended-on via npm.
   225|
   226|### template-nextjs-client
   227|15-page Next.js scaffold with 20+ components, used as base for new standalone sites. Contains: analytics, footer, header, cookie-consent, whatsapp-float, search-autocomplete, product-card, recently-viewed, wishlist, sitemap, robots, RSS.
   228|
   229|### paragu-ai-builder (the engine)
   230|- 1000+ business types in registry
   231|- 108 section components
   232|- 47 UI components
   233|- Supabase schema
   234|- Sites directory with 13 client data directories STILL inside
   235|
   236|---
   237|
   238|## 6. ACTION PLAN
   239|
   240|### Phase 1: Fix El Viajero (immediate)
   241|1. Remove `.orig` / `.rej` files (git conflicts)
   242|2. Verify build passes
   243|3. Push latest changes
   244|
   245|### Phase 2: Extract sites still in PAB (high priority)
   246|4 sites need extraction to standalone repos:
   247|- **Nexa Paraguay** — has web/app/nexa-paraguay routes, export
   248|- **Nexa Propiedades** — data only
   249|- **Nudo** — data only
   250|- **Stoicfinch** — data only
   251|- **De Abasto a Casa** — data only
   252|- **Bufete Méndez** — data only
   253|- **Polki Squad** — data only
   254|
   255|### Phase 3: Document every standalone repo
   256|Clone the template docs structure used by elviajero (CLAUDE.md, docs/brand-guide.md, docs/upgrade-plan.md, docs/client-questionnaire.md) to every repo that lacks them.
   257|
   258|Repos missing docs:
   259|- **Stroopwafel** — CLAUDE.md ✅, missing brand-guide, upgrade-plan
   260|- **Golden Visa** — CLAUDE.md ✅, missing brand-guide, upgrade-plan  
   261|- **Granja Cabral** — docs exist but not standardized
   262|- **Oz Montania** — CLAUDE.md ✅, missing brand-guide, upgrade-plan
   263|- **3MD** — missing standardized docs
   264|- **Depiflash** — CLAUDE.md ✅, missing brand-guide, upgrade-plan
   265|- **Fun4Me** — CLAUDE.md ✅, missing brand-guide
   266|- **Dayah** — CLAUDE.md ✅, should have upgrade-plan
   267|
   268|### Phase 4: Standardize shared components
   269|Despite the client-kit repo existing, no site installs from it. Two options:
   270|A) Fix npm auth (preferred but complex)
   271|B) Make the template the source of truth and sync from there
   272|
   273|### Phase 5: Cross-feature porting
   274|Port elviajero features down to other sites:
   275|1. **Cookie consent** → Golden Visa, Stroopwafel, Granja Cabral, Fun4Me
   276|2. **GA4** → Dayah (fix broken), all others
   277|3. **JSON-LD** → All sites (business schema + sitemap)
   278|4. **Sitemap + robots** → Sites that lack them
   279|5. **WhatsApp float** → Sites that lack it (Golden Visa, Stroopwafel)
   280|
   281|---
   282|
   283|## 7. El Viajero vs. Template — Delta
   284|
   285|What elviajero has that the template lacks:
   286|- Admin panel (10+ pages)
   287|- E-com full flow (cart, checkout, payments)
   288|- Auth (login, register, profile)
   289|- SQLite + Supabase backend
   290|- Product comparison
   291|- Multiple payment gateways (4)
   292|- Multi-language (3 locales)
   293|- Dark mode
   294|- Currency switcher (PYG/USD)
   295|- Image gallery / magnifier
   296|- Product reviews
   297|- Back-in-stock notifications
   298|- BOGO coupon system
   299|- Abandoned cart recovery
   300|- Exit intent popup
   301|- Recently viewed (both versions)
   302|- Shipping calculator
   303|- Tax display
   304|- Order tracking
   305|
   306|What elviajero needs (gaps):
   307|- Latest template components (product-card, quick-order, payment-methods)
   308|- Client-kit sync (some copies may be stale)
   309|- Real product photos (currently SVGs/Unsplash)
   310|
   311|---
   312|
   313|## 8. File Cleanup
   314|
   315|Files to remove:
   316|- `/root/elviajero-comercio/components/header.tsx.orig` — git merge artifact
   317|- `/root/elviajero-comercio/components/header.tsx.rej` — git merge artifact
   318|- `/root/elviajero-comercio/app/tienda/page.tsx.part` — partial file
   319|
   320|Files to verify:
   321|- `/root/elviajero-comercio/components/cart-sidebar.tsx` — check for `params` prop (common error)
   322|- `/root/elviajero-comercio/lib/supabase/client.ts` — check env vars
   323|", "total_lines": 322, "file_size": 12930, "truncated": false, "is_binary": false, "is_image": false}

# Incident Commander Skill

You are an autonomous Site Reliability Engineer. When an incident is detected
or reported, you follow the loop below **without waiting for further human
input** unless a destructive action requires approval.

## Core Incident Loop

```
DETECT → TRIAGE → DIAGNOSE → REMEDIATE → VERIFY → DOCUMENT → LEARN
```

### 1. DETECT
Gather signals immediately. Run all diagnostics in parallel via subagents
when possible:

```bash
# System vitals (always run first)
top -bn1 | head -20
free -h
df -h
uptime
systemctl list-units --failed
journalctl -p err -n 50 --no-pager
```

### 2. TRIAGE — Severity Classification

| Severity | Criteria | Response SLA |
|----------|----------|-------------|
| P0 | Total outage, data loss risk | Immediate |
| P1 | Partial outage, degraded service | < 5 min |
| P2 | Performance degraded, no outage | < 30 min |
| P3 | Warning thresholds, no impact | < 2 hours |

Announce severity via gateway immediately after triage.

### 3. DIAGNOSE — Root Cause Analysis

**High CPU:**
```bash
ps aux --sort=-%cpu | head -20
strace -p <pid> -c -e trace=all 2>&1 | head -30
lsof -p <pid> | wc -l
```

**Memory pressure:**
```bash
cat /proc/meminfo
ps aux --sort=-%mem | head -20
cat /proc/<pid>/status | grep -E "VmRSS|VmPeak|OomScore"
```

**Disk full:**
```bash
du -sh /* 2>/dev/null | sort -rh | head -20
find / -name "*.log" -size +100M 2>/dev/null
lsof | grep deleted | awk '{print $7, $9}' | sort -rn | head -10
```

**Service crash:**
```bash
systemctl status <service> -l --no-pager
journalctl -u <service> -n 100 --no-pager
```

**Docker container issues:**
```bash
docker ps -a
docker stats --no-stream
docker logs <container> --tail 100
```

### 4. REMEDIATE — Self-Healing Actions

Execute fixes in order of safety (least-destructive first):

**Tier 1 — Safe (no approval needed):**
- Clear temp files and old logs
- Restart failed non-critical services
- Adjust kernel parameters (sysctl)
- Kill runaway processes (non-PID-1)

**Tier 2 — Moderate (warn user, proceed after 30s unless cancelled):**
- Restart critical services
- Rollback last deployment
- Scale resources (if cloud API available)

**Tier 3 — Destructive (explicit approval required):**
- Data deletion
- Node termination
- Database operations

### 5. VERIFY — Confirm Resolution

Run the same diagnostics as step 1. Compare before/after metrics.
Declare resolution only when:
- All previously failed checks now pass
- Error rate returns to baseline
- Service response time is normal

### 6. DOCUMENT — Post-Incident Report

Always write a structured report to `~/.hermes/incidents/<timestamp>-<slug>.md`:

```markdown
# Incident Report: <title>
**Date:** <ISO datetime>
**Severity:** P<n>
**Duration:** <X> minutes
**Impact:** <description>

## Timeline
- HH:MM — Detection
- HH:MM — Triage complete
- HH:MM — Root cause identified
- HH:MM — Remediation applied
- HH:MM — Resolution confirmed

## Root Cause
<clear technical explanation>

## Remediation Steps
1. <step>
2. <step>

## Prevention
<what to change to prevent recurrence>

## Metrics
- MTTD (Mean Time to Detect): X min
- MTTR (Mean Time to Resolve): X min
```

### 7. LEARN — Skill Auto-Creation

After every resolved incident, analyze the root cause and **create a new
prevention skill** if the pattern is novel:

```python
# Template: ~/.hermes/skills/<incident-type>-prevention/SKILL.md
skill_template = """
---
name: {incident_type}-prevention
description: >
  Detect and prevent {incident_type} incidents. Activate when monitoring
  detects {trigger_conditions}.
---
# {incident_type} Prevention

## Early Warning Signs
{warning_signs}

## Automated Checks
{checks}

## Remediation Playbook
{playbook}
"""
```

## Cron Health Checks

When asked to set up monitoring, install these cron jobs:

```
# Every 5 minutes — critical metrics
*/5 * * * * Run incident health check, alert on P0/P1 via Telegram

# Every hour — comprehensive audit  
0 * * * * Run full system audit, save report to ~/.hermes/incidents/

# Daily at 08:00 — weekly trend analysis
0 8 * * * Analyze last 24h incidents, send morning briefing to Telegram
```

## Subagent Parallelism

For multi-service environments, spawn parallel subagents:

```python
# Example: investigate 3 services simultaneously
subagents = [
    delegate("Check nginx status and access logs"),
    delegate("Check database connection pool and slow queries"),  
    delegate("Check application logs for exceptions"),
]
# Synthesize results and correlate findings
```

## Memory Usage

After each incident, update MEMORY.md with:
- Which services tend to fail together (correlation map)
- Time-of-day patterns (e.g., "high CPU every weekday 9-10am")
- Which remediations worked vs. didn't
- Infrastructure topology learned over time

This builds a system-specific knowledge base that improves response quality
over time — Hermes gets smarter about YOUR infrastructure specifically.

## Notification Templates

**P0 Alert (Telegram):**
```
🚨 P0 INCIDENT DECLARED
Service: <name>
Impact: <description>
Started: <time>
Hermes is investigating. Updates every 60s.
```

**Resolution Notice:**
```
✅ INCIDENT RESOLVED
Duration: X minutes
Root cause: <summary>
MTTR: X min | Full report: ~/.hermes/incidents/<file>
```

## Integration Points

- **Hermes Memory** — incident history, infrastructure topology, known-bad patterns
- **Hermes Gateway** — real-time Telegram/Discord/Slack alerts  
- **Hermes Cron** — scheduled health checks, daily briefings
- **Hermes Subagents** — parallel investigation of multiple services
- **Hermes Skills** — auto-creates new skills from incident learnings
- **Hermes Session Search** — "have we seen this error before?"

# 🔥 El Viajero Tienda — Complete Roast & Deep Analysis

## CRO Score: **3.5 / 10**

This is a catalog site pretending to be an e-commerce store. It has the *skeleton* of a proper shop — cart, checkout, search, filters — but every single layer has fundamental problems that would kill conversions for a real Paraguayan customer trying to spend money.

---

## 1. 🖼️ IMAGES — CATASTROPHIC PERFORMANCE FAILURE

**133 MB of PNG images. Zero WebP. Zero optimization.**

Every product image is 1.4–2.9 MB uncompressed PNG at 1536x1024. For a site serving Paraguayan mobile users on 3G/4G connections, this is sabotage.

- `hamaca.png` — 2.9 MB
- `guantes.png` — 2.8 MB  
- `caja-pesca.png` — 2.6 MB
- 82 PNG files, 0 WebP files

A user on a typical Paraguayan 4G connection (~10 Mbps) loads the tienda page and needs to download **30+ MB of product images** just to see the catalog. That's 25+ seconds of white space on mobile. They're gone.

**Fix:** Convert all to WebP, serve via `<picture>` with `srcset`, add `loading="lazy"` (the code does use `SafeImage` but the source files are absurd). Target under 100KB per product image.

---

## 2. 🏗️ ARCHITECTURE — DUAL DATA SOURCE CHAOS

The tienda page has a **dangerous dual-data architecture**:

```typescript
// First: tries Supabase DB
const allProducts = dbProducts.length > 0 ? dbProducts : staticProducts
```

**Problem:** If Supabase is slow, down, or returns an error (not `data.length === 0` but an actual network error), the silently falls back to the static JSON fallback. The products shown could be completely different from what's actually in stock. There's no error handling — just `if (data && data.length > 0)` with no `else` for the error case.

Even worse: **prices in the JSON are strings** (`"Gs. 450.000"`). The code then does `parseInt(s.replace(/[^\\d]/g, ""))` to parse prices everywhere — in the filter component, in the product card, in the checkout. This is brittle, locale-dependent, and will break the moment someone writes a price differently.

**Fix:** Prices should be stored as integers (cents/guaraníes). Parse once, format for display.

---

## 3. 🛒 CHECKOUT — THE CONVERSION KILLER

This is where the roast gets serious. The checkout flow has **three massive problems**:

### 3a. Hardcoded WhatsApp Number
```typescript
window.location.href = `https://wa.me/[REDACTED_PHONE]?text=${msg}`
```
The WhatsApp number is hardcoded in the checkout page, duplicated in `es.json`, and baked into the layout structured data. If Osmar changes his number, you're hunting through 5+ files.

### 3b. "WhatsApp/Transferencia" is the DEFAULT payment method
The primary payment flow literally redirects the user OUT of the site to WhatsApp with a pre-filled message. The customer fills out a 3-step checkout form, clicks "Confirmar pedido", and gets bounced to WhatsApp where they have to RE-EXPLAIN their order. The order is saved to the DB but the user experience is: fill form → leave site → start a WhatsApp conversation. 

**This is not checkout. This is a contact form with extra steps.**

### 3c. Pagopar and Bancard are listed but...
There's a `/api/checkout` route that handles these, but the implementation is minimal. If either fails, the user sees "Error al procesar el pago" with no recovery path. No retry, no saved cart state.

### 3d. No form validation
The checkout has `required` HTML attributes on inputs but zero client-side validation. The "Continuar" button just does `setStep(2)` regardless of whether fields are filled. A customer can reach Step 3 with an empty name, invalid email, and no phone.

---

## 4. 🔍 SEARCH & FILTERS — STATE MANAGEMENT BUG

The filter component has a **re-render anti-pattern**:

```typescript
// We call it via setTimeout to avoid re-render loops
setTimeout(() => onFilteredProducts(filtered), 0)
```

This is a code smell that screams "we had infinite re-render bugs and slapped setTimeout on it." The `filtered` array is computed via `useMemo` but then passed to the parent via setTimeout callback instead of being returned properly. This creates:
- **Flash of unfiltered content** on every filter change (parent still shows old state)
- **Race conditions** if user changes filters rapidly
- **Impossible to test** deterministically

Plus the `useMemo` dependency array is missing `brandFilter` and `pricePreset`:
```typescript
}, [search, categoryFilter, sortBy, priceMin, priceMax, stockFilter, products])
// Missing: brandFilter, pricePreset
```

Brand filtering literally doesn't update the memo. Users select "FishMaster" and... nothing happens until some other filter changes.

---

## 5. 📱 SEO — THE TIENDA PAGE HAS ZERO SEO

```bash
$ grep -rn 'metadata\|generateMetadata' /root/elviajero/app/tienda/
# (empty — nothing)
```

The tienda page exports no metadata. No `<title>`, no `<meta description>`, no Open Graph, no structured data for products. The only SEO on the entire site is in the root layout — a generic "El Viajero — Tu Aventura Empieza Acá" that applies to every single page.

For Google Shopping, Product rich results, or even basic SERP visibility for "camping paraguay" or "pesca paraguay" — this page is invisible. Individual product pages (`/producto/[slug]`) also lack `generateMetadata`.

**Fix:** Every product page needs `generateMetadata()` with Product schema (price, availability, image, offers). The tienda page needs CollectionPage schema.

---

## 6. 🧭 NAVIGATION — CONFUSING DUPLICATES

The `es.json` has TWO navigation arrays:

```json
"items": [ /* 8 items including "Productos" → /productos */ ],
"ui":    [ /* 7 items WITHOUT Productos, with Tienda → /tienda */ ]
```

So there's `/tienda` (the main catalog page) AND `/productos` (a separate page). The user sees "Tienda" in the main nav but the JSON also has "Productos" pointing to `/productos`. This is either a leftover from a refactor or an intentional split that confuses users.

Are they the same? Different? The nav shows "Tienda" but the JSON metadata says "Productos → /productos". This is a breadcrumb that leads nowhere useful.

---

## 7. 🏷️ BRAND NAMES — OBVIOUSLY FAKE

Look at the brand list:
> AgriTool, AirGo, AutoVue, BeachRest, BreezeGo, BrightLight, CampMaster, CoolKing, CoolPack, FarmLife, FishMaster, FishMate, FloatLine, GrillGo, GripPro...

These are AI-generated compound words. Not a single one is a real brand. Every product page says "SleepWell", "FishMaster", "OutdoorPro" — brands that don't exist. For a Paraguayan customer who knows their local market, this is an immediate trust killer. "These people made up their own brands" = "they probably made up the products too."

Even if these are meant to be placeholder brands from a wholesaler, they should be transparent about it — or use the actual manufacturer names.

---

## 8. 💰 PRICING — STRING PARSING HELL

Every price is stored as `"Gs. 450.000"` (a formatted string with dots as thousand separators). This means:

- Sorting by price requires regex parsing: `parseInt(s.replace(/[^\d]/g, ""))`
- USD conversion has to parse the same string again
- Checkout calculates totals by re-parsing strings
- If anyone types a price without the dots, or with commas, or as "450000" — it breaks silently

The `PriceUSD` component does the conversion client-side on every render. With 34 products visible, that's 34 string parses per page load just for the USD display.

---

## 9. 🎨 UI/UX ISSUES (from the accessibility snapshot)

### 9a. Hero section is generic
```
h1: "Tienda Online"
subtitle: "Compra desde casa y recibi en tu puerta"
```
Zero personality. "Tienda Online" is what every generic shop says. No value proposition, no urgency, no emotional hook.

### 9b. Filter bar is overwhelming
6 filter controls + price preset buttons + stock checkbox — all visible simultaneously. For a catalog with only 34 products, this is overkill. The category, brand, and availability filters could easily be collapsible.

### 9c. No urgency or social proof
- No customer reviews visible on the catalog
- No "X personas están viendo esto" 
- No "Vendido 23 veces"
- No testimonial or trust badges on the tienda page
- The "OFERTA" badge only shows if there's a `priceBefore` — but most products don't have one
- Stock badges exist but only show for ≤5 units — the rest look identical

### 9d. Product cards are data-dense but emotion-empty
```
Carpa 4 Personas
4 pers | 3 kg | 210x210x130cm | Impermeable 2000mm
Gs. 450.000  ≈ USD 60.8
[Agregar]
```
Specs pipe-delimited in tiny text. No benefit copy. "4 pers | 3 kg" tells me specs, not WHY I want this tent. Compare: "Tent familiar para 4, ultraliviana, impermeable — lista en 5 minutos."

### 9e. Dual CartProvider wrapping
The tienda page wraps `<CartProvider>` inside its own page component, but the root `layout.tsx` ALSO wraps `<CartProvider>`. Double-wrapped React context means the inner cart state is isolated from the rest of the app. Add something in tienda → navigate to another page → cart might be empty.

---

## 10. 🏗️ CODE QUALITY ISSUES

| Issue | Severity |
|-------|----------|
| `setTimeout` callback state hack in filters | 🔴 Critical |
| Missing `brandFilter` and `pricePreset` in useMemo deps | 🔴 Critical |
| Double `CartProvider` wrapping (layout + page) | 🟡 Medium |
| `content as any` used everywhere (zero type safety) | 🟡 Medium |
| Prices as formatted strings, parsed everywhere | 🟡 Medium |
| No error handling on Supabase queries | 🟡 Medium |
| No form validation in checkout | 🟡 Medium |
| No `generateMetadata` on any page except root | 🔴 SEO Critical |
| `supabase` created inside component (not memoized) | 🟠 Low-Med |
| `slugify` function duplicated across 3 files | 🟢 Low |

---

## 11. WHAT'S ACTUALLY GOOD

Fair credit where due:
- **Design system is consistent** — proper Tailwind v4 theme variables, dark mode support
- **Feature-rich** — 33 API routes, 114 components, full admin panel, B2B portal, blog, reviews, wishlist, recently viewed, cart recovery, stock alerts
- **i18n ready** — ES/EN/GN language toggle with proper content structure
- **Currency toggle** — PYG/USD with live conversion
- **Structured data** on root layout — Store schema with proper address, hours, payment methods
- **Supabase RLS** — proper row-level security on all tables
- **Cookie consent** — GDPR-ready even for a Paraguayan shop
- **The checkout shipping zones are correct** — Asunción/Central/Interior/Pickup with proper thresholds

---

## 12. PRIORITY FIX LIST (by revenue impact)

| # | Fix | Impact |
|---|-----|--------|
| 1 | Convert all images to WebP, compress to <100KB each | **Performance: 30→2s load** |
| 2 | Add `generateMetadata` to tienda + all product pages | **SEO: from invisible to indexable** |
| 3 | Fix useMemo missing dependencies (brand filter broken) | **Functional: brand filter literally doesn't work** |
| 4 | Remove duplicate CartProvider | **Cart items disappearing on navigation** |
| 5 | Add form validation to checkout | **Customers submitting empty orders** |
| 6 | Extract WhatsApp number to env/config | **Maintainability** |
| 7 | Store prices as integers, format for display | **Code quality, prevent parsing bugs** |
| 8 | Rewrite hero with actual value proposition | **CRO: first impression** |
| 9 | Add social proof / trust signals to tienda page | **CRO: trust building** |
| 10 | Replace fake brand names with real ones or explain | **Trust: customers aren't stupid** |

The site is **architecturally ambitious** for a PYME — the component count and feature set rival stores 10x the size. But the fundamentals that actually drive sales — speed, trust, and a frictionless path to purchase — are all compromised. It's a Ferrari engine in a car with no tires.", "timestamp": [REDACTED_PHONE].9068727, "anchor": true}, {"id": 157361, "role": "user", "content": "fix all of thios ", "timestamp": [REDACTED_PHONE].3996322}], "bookend_end": [], "messages_before": 5, "messages_after": 1}], "count": 3, "sessions_searched": 3}

{"content":"# El Viajero - Client Feedback & Requirements Tracker

Last updated: [REDACTED_PHONE]

---

## Current Status
- **Live:** el-viajero.paragu-ai.com | viajero.paragu-ai.com
- **Repo:** /root/elviajero (elviajero-comercio on GitHub)
- **Stack:** Next.js 15, Tailwind 4, TypeScript, Supabase + SQLite
- **Maturity:** HIGH - 107 components, full e-commerce, admin panel

---

## Known Issues & Feedback

### 1. 🚨 Product Page 500 Error (FIXED [REDACTED_PHONE])
**Status:** ✅ RESOLVED
**Issue:** Product pages showing "Error del servidor" + React error #310 (infinite re-render)
**Root Cause:** `createClient()` called on every render, causing new Supabase reference. useEffect had `[supabase]` dependency → infinite loop.
**Fix:** Changed useEffect dependency from `[supabase]` to `[]` so it runs once on mount.
**Deployed:** Commit 1ab554e, GitHub Actions deploying.

### 2. 🖼️ Image Performance (from CRO Roast)
**Status:** ⚠️ NOT ADDRESSED
**Issue:** 133 MB of PNG images, 0 WebP. Product images are 1.4–2.9 MB each.
**Impact:** Mobile users on 3G/4G connections download 30+ MB just to load catalog = 25+ seconds white space.
**Fix Required:** Convert all to WebP, serve via `<picture>` with `srcset`, add `loading="lazy"`. Target <100KB per image.

### 3. 🏗️ Dual Data Source Chaos (from CRO Roast)
**Status:** ⚠️ NOT ADDRESSED
**Issue:** Product data tries Supabase first, silently falls back to static JSON on error. Prices stored as formatted strings ("Gs. 450.000") requiring regex parsing everywhere.
**Impact:** If Supabase down/slow, products shown may not match actual inventory. Price parsing brittle, locale-dependent.
**Fix Required:**
- Store prices as integers (cents/guaraníes)
- Parse once, format for display
- Proper error handling for Supabase queries

### 4. 🛒 Checkout Problems (from CRO Roast)
**Status:** ⚠️ NOT ADDRESSED
**Issues:**
- Hardcoded WhatsApp number (`[REDACTED_PHONE]`) in checkout page
- "WhatsApp/Transferencia" is DEFAULT payment - redirects user to WhatsApp instead of completing checkout
- Bancard/PagoPar listed but minimal implementation
- No form validation - user can submit empty order
**Fix Required:**
- Extract WhatsApp number to env/config
- Implement real payment gateway completion flow
- Add form validation before proceeding

### 5. 🔍 Search & Filters Bug (from CRO Roast)
**Status:** ⚠️ NOT ADDRESSED
**Issue:** Filter component has `setTimeout` callback hack (re-render anti-pattern). `useMemo` missing `brandFilter` and `pricePreset` dependencies.
**Impact:** Brand filter doesn't update memo. Flash of unfiltered content, race conditions on rapid filter changes.
**Fix Required:**
- Remove `setTimeout` callback, return from `useMemo` properly
- Add `brandFilter, pricePreset` to dependency array

### 6. 📱 SEO - No Metadata (from CRO Roast)
**Status:** ⚠️ NOT ADDRESSED
**Issue:** Tienda page exports no metadata. No `<title>`, no meta description, no Open Graph, no structured data.
**Impact:** Invisible to Google Shopping, Product rich results, basic SERP visibility.
**Fix Required:**
- Add `generateMetadata()` to tienda page with CollectionPage schema
- Add `generateMetadata()` to all product pages with Product schema (price, availability, image, offers)

### 7. 🧭 Navigation Confusion (from CRO Roast)
**Status:** ⚠️ NOT ADDRESSED
**Issue:** `es.json` has TWO navigation arrays - "items" with Productos→/productos, "ui" with Tienda→/tienda.
**Impact:** User sees "Tienda" in nav but breadcrumb leads nowhere useful or confused routing.

### 8. 💰 Price String Parsing Hell (from CRO Roast)
**Status:** ⚠️ NOT ADDRESSED
**Issue:** Every price stored as "Gs. 450.000" string with thousand separators. Requires `parseInt(s.replace(/[^\d]/g, ""))` everywhere.
**Fix Required:** Store as integers, format for display only.

---

## Feature Requests (from Build Wishlist)

### Tier 2.3 - El Viajero Polish
**Priority:** 🟢 SOON
**Estimated:** 24h

| Feature | Notes |
|---------|--------|
| Supabase migration | Currently SQLite - fragile for multi-replica Docker. Migrate to Supabase for durability. |
| Real payment gateway | Bancard vPOS integration - complete checkout flow without WhatsApp redirect |
| Stock sync | Reconcile online store with physical shop inventory |
| Auto-order to WhatsApp | New orders → auto-forward to shop WhatsApp ([REDACTED_PHONE]) |
| Delivery tracking | Real-time courier integration |
| Product variant matrix | Size × color × material × price per variant |

---

## What's Working ✅

- Full e-commerce flow (cart, checkout, orders, shipping, coupons)
- Multi-payment gateway (Bancard, PagoPar, PayPal, Stripe)
- Full admin panel (10+ pages: products, orders, categories, users, coupons, reviews, etc.)
- SEO complete (sitemap, JSON-LD, RSS, OG, robots) - on root layout
- Analytics (GA4)
- Auth system (custom pg JWT)
- Cart persistence
- Multi-currency (PYG/USD) with live conversion
- Multi-language (ES/EN/GN locales)
- Dark mode
- Cookie consent
- Product comparison
- Recently viewed + wishlist + back-in-stock
- BOGO + bundles auto-promos
- Exit intent + abandoned cart recovery
- Blog engine

---

## Next Actions

1. **Immediate** (blocked by deploy):
   - Verify product page fix is live (check https://el-viajero.paragu-ai.com/producto/*)
   - Monitor for any re-render errors in browser console

2. **High Priority** (from CRO roast + wishlist):
   - Convert images to WebP, optimize to <100KB each
   - Add SEO metadata to tienda page and all product pages
   - Fix search/filter useMemo dependencies (brand filter broken)
   - Remove dual navigation confusion
   - Implement real checkout flow (not WhatsApp redirect)
   - Fix price storage as integers

3. **Medium Priority** (from wishlist):
   - Supabase migration (replace SQLite)
   - Stock sync system
   - Auto-order to WhatsApp
   - Delivery tracking
   - Product variant matrix

---

## Sources

- `/root/priorities/repos/elviajero-deep-analysis.md` - Architecture analysis
- `/root/priorities/build-wishlist.md` - All client requests
- CRO Roast session 20260512 - Live site audit (from search results)
- Supabase schema `supabase/migrations/002_ej_tables.sql` - Product table structure
- Current codebase `/root/elviajero/`

{"content": "     1|# El Viajero — Brutal Asset Critique & Complete Upgrade Plan
     2|
     3|## Executive Summary
     4|
     5|We have ~82 images. ~60 are fine (hero carousel, product PNGs, promo images). 
     6|**~15 assets are embarrassing.** This doc roasts each one, then prescribes the fix.
     7|
     8|---
     9|
    10|## THE ROAST
    11|
    12|### 1. Logo — `public/images/logo.svg`
    13|
    14|**Current state:** 200x60 SVG. A tiny mountain with a tent crammed into the left side. "El Viajero" text in a font that probably doesn't render on most systems (Poppins). Tagline "Todo para tu aventura" is microscopic at 10px. The mountain gradient goes from green to blue like someone couldn't decide on a brand color. The sun is a tiny yellow dot.
    15|
    16|**Problems:**
    17|- The icon is too small (28px) — invisible on mobile
    18|- Uses Poppins which 90% of users don't have — falls back to ugly sans-serif
    19|- The tent looks like an afterthought crammed under the mountain
    20|- No brand recognition — could be any outdoor store
    21|- Green-to-blue gradient is wrong — we're Camping/Forest, not Beach/Ocean
    22|
    23|**Fix:** Complete redesign. Needs:
    24|- Clean, bold icon that works at 32px (header) and 200px (OG image)
    25|- Outdoor/Travel feel without being generic
    26|- Must render well without web fonts (use system fonts or inline paths)
    27|- Brand mark should work standalone (without text)
    28|
    29|---
    30|
    31|### 2. Favicon — `public/images/favicon.svg`
    32|
    33|**Current state:** Green rounded rect with white "EV" text. Looks like a generic app icon from 2012. Zero personality.
    34|
    35|**Problems:**
    36|- "EV" means nothing to customers
    37|- Looks like a banking app
    38|- Doesn't convey camping, adventure, or Paraguay
    39|- When tiled in browser tabs, blends into nothing
    40|
    41|**Fix:** A minimalist mountain peak + tent icon @64x64. Must be recognizable at 16x16 browser tab size.
    42|
    43|---
    44|
    45|### 3. OG Image — `public/images/og-viajero.svg`
    46|
    47|**Current state:** 1200x630 gradient (green → blue). "EV" in a white rounded box. Category tags using emojis. Looks like a template from 2018.
    48|
    49|**Problems:**
    50|- The "EV" logo square is laughably basic — white rounded rect with opacity
    51|- Emoji in OG images render differently on every platform (Twitter vs Facebook vs WhatsApp)
    52|- Mountain decoration paths are random noise — not recognizable shapes
    53|- URL at bottom-right is tiny and useless
    54|- No call-to-action or visual hook
    55|
    56|**Fix:** Needs real brand photography or a polished illustration. Typography hierarchy: Brand → Value Prop → Location → CTA.
    57|
    58|---
    59|
    60|### 4-9. Category SVGs — 6 files in `public/images/categories/`
    61|
    62|**Current state (BEFORE our upgrade):** 400x267 SVGs with emoji text (🏕️🎣🎒🚗🏍️🌾) on a light green background. It looked like an SMS message.
    63|
    64|**Our fix:** We replaced them with vector illustrations. Better, but still not great.
    65|
    66|**Remaining problems:**
    67|- They're 400x267 but displayed as 20% opacity backgrounds — detail gets lost
    68|- The category-layout.tsx renders an `<Image>` with `fill` + `object-cover` at `opacity-20` — the photo is barely visible under the green overlay
    69|- Need richer, higher-contrast images that work through a dark overlay
    70|
    71|**Fix:** Replace all 6 with high-contrast photo-realistic backgrounds. Darker tones, stronger silhouettes, so they're visible at 20% opacity through a `bg-primary/82` overlay.
    72|
    73|---
    74|
    75|### 10-11. PWA Icons — `icon-192.png`, `icon-512.png`
    76|
    77|**Current state:** Simple green rounded rect with white mountain + tent + sun. Generated via Pillow.
    78|
    79|**Problems:**
    80|- Generated programmatically — jagged edges, no anti-aliasing
    81|- The mountain is a basic polygon — looks like clip art
    82|- When a user adds the site to their home screen, the icon looks amateurish
    83|- No iOS/Android adaptive icon support
    84|
    85|**Fix:** Properly rendered SVG → PNG conversion at correct sizes. Or a dedicated PWA icon set that mirrors the main logo.
    86|
    87|---
    88|
    89|### 12-17. Category Hero Banners — 6 files in `public/images/marketing/`
    90|
    91|**Current state:** Photorealistic-style PNGs (1920x600) we just generated. Gradient sky + bokeh circles + basic silhouettes.
    92|
    93|**Problems:**
    94|- The "photorealistic" claim is generous — they're gradients with random circles
    95|- No actual subjects (tents, fishing rods, cars) — just atmosphere
    96|- They work OK as hero backgrounds but won't impress anyone
    97|- For the price of the "real" ones, we could have generated proper AI images
    98|
    99|**Fix:** These are acceptable as placeholders but need real AI-generated images when the budget allows.
   100|
   101|---
   102|
   103|### 18. `public/images/og-viajero.svg` (OG Image)
   104|
   105|Already criticized above. Worth repeating: this is the image that shows up when you share the site on WhatsApp/Facebook/Twitter. It's **the most important image** for social proof, and it's embarrassing.
   106|
   107|---
   108|
   109|## THE UPGRADE PLAN
   110|
   111|### Phase 1: Low Effort, High Impact (1 hour)
   112|
   113|| # | What | Effort | Impact | Action |
   114||---|------|--------|--------|--------|
   115|| 1 | Logo | 20min | High | AI-generate a proper outdoor/travel brand mark — mountain with path/trail motif, warm earthy colors, works at 32px |
   116|| 2 | Favicon | 10min | High | Extract a simplified version of the new logo — must read at 16x16 |
   117|| 3 | Category hero banners | 30min | Medium | Regenerate with actual subjects (not just gradients) |
   118|
   119|### Phase 2: Medium Effort (2 hours)
   120|
   121|| # | What | Effort | Impact | Action |
   122||---|------|--------|--------|--------|
   123|| 4 | OG Image | 30min | High | Full redesign with brand photography, proper typography, CTA section |
   124|| 5 | Category SVGs | 30min | Medium | Replace vector illustrations with photo-realistic composited images |
   125|| 6 | PWA Icons | 15min | Medium | Generate clean set from SVG logo, all required sizes |
   126|
   127|### Phase 3: Long-term (client provides)
   128|
   129|| # | What | Effort | Impact | Action |
   130||---|------|--------|--------|--------|
   131|| 7 | Real product photos | Client | High | Replace all 34 placeholder PNGs with actual product photos |
   132|| 8 | Store photography | Client | Medium | Real photos of storefront, interior, team for /nosotros |
   133|| 9 | Customer testimonial photos | Client | Low | Real customer photos replacing our avatar placeholders |
   134|
   135|---
   136|
   137|## Detailed Design Brief for Each Asset
   138|
   139|### Logo Redesign
   140|
   141|**Concept:** Mountain peak with a glowing trail or path that forms a "V" shape (for Viajero). Earthy green (#1B5E20) and warm gold (#E65100). The icon should work at 32x32 and 200x200.
   142|
   143|**Reference style:** Patagonia (bold, clean) meets a Paraguayan mate gourd silhouette.
   144|
   145|**Colors:** #1B5E20 (primary green), #E65100 (accent orange), #37474F (dark text)
   146|
   147|### OG Image Redesign
   148|
   149|**Layout:**
   150|- Left 40%: Large mountain/tent illustration or photo
   151|- Right 60%: Brand name + tagline + 3 category pills + location
   152|- Bottom: URL with decorative wave
   153|- No emoji — everything as rendered SVG text/paths
   154|
   155|### Category Backgrounds
   156|
   157|Each needs a photo-composite feel at 1920x600:
   158|- Camping: Forest canopy with tent glow, moonlight
   159|- Pesca: River/riverbank at golden hour, mist
   160|- Outdoor: Mountain trail with backpack, morning light
   161|- Autos: Highway at sunset, dashboard reflection
   162|- Motos: Dirt road through fields, afternoon sun
   163|- Campo: Rural farmland, rolling hills, farmhouse silhouette
   164|
   165|---
   166|
   167|## What We Should Generate Right Now
   168|
   169|| Priority | Asset | Format | Where to use |
   170||----------|-------|--------|-------------|
   171|| P1 | Full brand logo | SVG | Header, favicon, PWA, OG |
   172|| P1 | Favicon | SVG/PNG | Browser tab, bookmarks |
   173|| P2 | OG image | PNG | Social sharing |
   174|| P2 | Category hero banners | PNG | Category pages |
   175|| P3 | Category SVGs | SVG | Category page hero decorative |
   176|| P3 | PWA icons | PNG | Home screen, splash screen |
   177|", "total_lines": 176, "file_size": 7505, "truncated": false, "is_binary": false, "is_image": false}

     2|
     3|## Live
     4|https://el-viajero.paragu-ai.com (HTTP 200, 2 Docker replicas, Docker Swarm)

{"content": "     1|# El Viajero — Upgrade Plan ([REDACTED_PHONE])
     2|
     3|> Prepared for: Osmar Aguilera
     4|> Live site: https://el-viajero.paragu-ai.com
     5|> Report for: Kiki / Hermes Agent
     6|
     7|---
     8|
     9|## 1. ALREADY DONE (from git log)
    10|
    11|- [x] **Logo & Branding** — SVG logo, PNG OG image, favicon, PWA icons, hero banners, category SVGs
    12|- [x] **Admin Panel** — Full redesign with emerald/zinc theme, sidebar, Lucide icons, loading states, skeletons, DataTable, bulk import, search, pagination
    13|- [x] **Auth** — Supabase Auth (email/password), custom login/register pages, admin role check, session persistence, password reset route
    14|- [x] **Supabase Migration** — Full schema (ej_products, ej_orders, ej_reviews, ej_categories, ej_promo_codes, etc.), 34 seeded products, RLS policies
    15|- [x] **Refactoring** — Auth context split (4 modules), header split (5 subcomponents), admin layout split, payment gateway factory, DB CRUD factory, storage-keys, types extraction, supabase client cleanup
    16|- [x] **Cart System** — CartContext + CartProvider with localStorage persistence, cart badge, cart sidebar, "Agregar" button on /tienda, save-for-later, toast notifications, share cart, reorder button
    17|- [x] **Checkout Page** — Full checkout flow with shipping zones, address form, Supabase order creation, Pagopar/Bancard/Stripe/WhatsApp gateway routing
    18|- [x] **Product Detail Page** — `/producto/[slug]` route with image gallery, add-to-cart, quantity selector, WhatsApp inquiry, Product schema JSON-LD
    19|- [x] **Search** — Search overlay with autocomplete, product name/brand search, debounced
    20|- [x] **Admin Features** — Products (CRUD + bulk), orders (CRUD), customers, reviews, promos, content editor, blog, categories, B2B, theme editor, stats dashboard, subscribers, enrich AI
    21|- [x] **Homepage** — Hero carousel (5 slides), animated stats, new arrivals grid, featured products, testimonials, category icons, features section, newsletter, WhatsApp float
    22|- [x] **SEO** — MetadataBase, Open Graph, Twitter cards, JSON-LD (Store schema), sitemap, Google/Facebook verification, PWA manifest
    23|- [x] **Internationalization** — ES/EN/GN toggles, PYG/USD currency toggle
    24|- [x] **Design** — Brand colors (green #1B5E20, accent #E65100), Inter fonts, dark mode, responsive
    25|
    26|---
    27|
    28|## 2. CRITICAL BUGS (block production readiness)
    29|
    30|### B1. [CRITICAL] CartProvider not in root layout — add-to-cart broken on homepage
    31|
    32|**Problem:** `CartProvider` is only wrapped around `/tienda`, `/producto/[slug]`, `/checkout`, and `/` (homepage). It is NOT in `app/layout.tsx`. The `<CartBadge>` component in the header calls `useCart()` directly. Any page that includes `<Header>` but isn't wrapped with `CartProvider` will crash at runtime (e.g. `/blog`, `/nosotros`, `/contacto`, `/faq`, `/promociones`, `/login`, `/register`, `/admin/*`, `/mi-cuenta/*`, `/categoria/*`, `/recuperar`, etc.).
    33|
    34|**Fix:** Move `CartProvider` wrapping from individual page wrappers into `app/layout.tsx`.
    35|
    36|**File:** `app/layout.tsx` lines 75-78
    37|
    38|```diff
    39|  <body>
    40|    <ErrorBoundary>
    41|-     <CurrencyProvider>{children}</CurrencyProvider>
    42|+     <CurrencyProvider>
    43|+       <CartProvider>
    44|+         <ToastProvider>
    45|+           {children}
    46|+         </ToastProvider>
    47|+       </CartProvider>
    48|+     </CurrencyProvider>
    49|    </ErrorBoundary>
    50|  </body>
    51|```
    52|
    53|**Status:** [ ] NOT DONE
    54|
    55|---
    56|
    57|### B2. [CRITICAL] Stats show 0 on homepage
    58|
    59|**Problem:** The stats section on the homepage (`/app/page.tsx` lines 38, 92-102) reads from `content/es.json` static data. The JSON has placeholder values like `"0"` for categories, products, clients, and Paraguay. No Supabase query runs to count real data.
    60|
    61|**Files:** `app/page.tsx` line 38, `content/es.json` home.stats.items
    62|
    63|**Fix:** Replace static stats with real Supabase counts, or update the JSON with real numbers.
    64|
    65|**Status:** [ ] NOT DONE
    66|
    67|---
    68|
    69|### B3. [CRITICAL] Product images are still placeholders (SVG/PNG illustrations)
    70|
    71|**Problem:** Products use generated illustration images (`/images/marketing/products/*.png`) rather than real product photos. This kills trust for first-time visitors. The detail page also lacks a real image gallery.
    72|
    73|**Files:** `content/es.json` product.imageUrl fields, `public/images/marketing/products/*.png`
    74|
    75|**Status:** [ ] NOT DONE
    76|
    77|---
    78|
    79|### B4. [CRITICAL] No payment gateways connected — all fall back to WhatsApp
    80|
    81|**Problem:** The checkout API (`app/api/checkout/route.ts`) has full code for Pagopar, Bancard, and Stripe, but all three fall back to WhatsApp when their API keys are missing. The `.env` file lacks real `PAGOPAR_PUBLIC_KEY`, `PAGOPAR_PRIVATE_KEY`, `BANCARD_PUBLIC_KEY`, `BANCARD_PRIVATE_KEY`, and `STRIPE_SECRET_KEY`. Customers cannot pay online — they can only order via WhatsApp inquiry.
    82|
    83|**Files:** `app/api/checkout/route.ts` lines 41-48, 82-88, 117-123
    84|
    85|**Status:** [ ] NOT DONE
    86|
    87|---
    88|
    89|### B5. [CRITICAL] Search redirect / misrouting — navigation has "/productos" route pointing nowhere
    90|
    91|**Problem:** The header nav includes a `/productos` link (line 19 of `components/header.tsx`) that routes to `/app/productos/page.tsx`, which is likely a redirect or empty page. This confuses customers. The search overlay works but may misroute search results.
    92|
    93|**Files:** `components/header.tsx` line 19, `app/productos/page.tsx`
    94|
    95|**Status:** [ ] NOT DONE (needs investigation)
    96|
    97|---
    98|
    99|## 3. HIGH PRIORITY IMPROVEMENTS
   100|
   101|### H1. Move CartProvider to root layout
   102|Same as B1 above — this is the single highest-impact fix.
   103|
   104|**Files:** `app/layout.tsx`
   105|
   106|---
   107|
   108|### H2. Fix homepage stats with real numbers
   109|Update `content/es.json` with actual store data (e.g., +200 productos, +150 clientes, 7 categorías, Paraguay-wide delivery).
   110|
   111|**Files:** `content/es.json` home.stats.items
   112|
   113|---
   114|
   115|### H3. Add real product photos
   116|Replace placeholder PNG illustrations with real product photos. Either:
   117|- Upload real photos to Supabase storage bucket (`ej_product_images`)
   118|- Use high-quality Unsplash/stock photos as temporary replacements
   119|- Enable admin image upload (the bucket and component exist but are untested)
   120|
   121|**Files:** `content/es.json` product.imageUrl fields, admin image upload component
   122|
   123|---
   124|
   125|### H4. Connect at least one payment gateway (Pagopar priority #1)
   126|Pagopar is the dominant payment method in Paraguay. Osmar needs to:
   127|1. Create a Pagopar merchant account
   128|2. Add `PAGOPAR_PUBLIC_KEY` and `PAGOPAR_PRIVATE_KEY` to `.env.local` and `docker-compose.yml`
   129|3. Test the checkout flow end-to-end
   130|
   131|**Files:** `app/api/checkout/route.ts`, `.env.local`
   132|
   133|---
   134|
   135|### H5. Fix admin dashboard stats
   136|Admin dashboard (`/admin`) shows 0 for users, products, orders, revenue because it queries the `products` table instead of `ej_products`.
   137|
   138|**Files:** `app/admin/page.tsx` line 7
   139|
   140|---
   141|
   142|### H6. Add CartProvider + CartSidebar to layout so cart works everywhere
   143|See B1. This ensures the cart badge in the header doesn't crash on any page, and the cart sidebar is accessible site-wide.
   144|
   145|---
   146|
   147|### H7. Remove duplicate /productos nav link or redirect to /tienda
   148|The nav has both "Tienda" and "Productos" pointing to different routes. Consolidate.
   149|
   150|**Files:** `components/header.tsx` line 19
   151|
   152|---
   153|
   154|### H8. Enable passwordless/guest checkout
   155|Current flow forces account creation. Add guest checkout option and/or WhatsApp-only ordering without registration.
   156|
   157|**Files:** `app/checkout/page.tsx`, `app/login/page.tsx`
   158|
   159|---
   160|
   161|### H9. Add real phone number and WhatsApp number to config
   162|Update `config/site.json` and `content/es.json` with Osmar's real phone and WhatsApp number.
   163|
   164|**Files:** `config/site.json`, `content/es.json`
   165|
   166|---
   167|
   168|### H10. Configure Google Analytics (GA4)
   169|GA4 is referenced in code but uses placeholder ID. Add real `NEXT_PUBLIC_GA_ID`.
   170|
   171|**Files:** `.env.local`, `docker-compose.yml`
   172|
   173|---
   174|
   175|## 4. MEDIUM PRIORITY IMPROVEMENTS
   176|
   177|### M1. Enable Google/Facebook OAuth in Supabase
   178|Buttons show but OAuth isn't configured. Osmar needs to add Client ID/Secret in Supabase dashboard.
   179|
   180|**Files:** Supabase Dashboard > Auth Providers
   181|
   182|---
   183|
   184|### M2. Add real phone number to WhatsApp CTAs
   185|All WhatsApp links use placeholder `[REDACTED_PHONE]`. Replace with Osmar's real number.
   186|
   187|**Files:** `content/es.json`, `config/site.json`, various components
   188|
   189|---
   190|
   191|### M3. Set up email sending (Resend)
   192|Cart recovery, order confirmations, and password reset emails need a configured Resend API key.
   193|
   194|**Files:** `.env.local` → add `RESEND_API_KEY`
   195|
   196|---
   197|
   198|### M4. Add breadcrumbs to all pages
   199|No breadcrumb navigation exists. Add for SEO and UX, especially on /producto/[slug] and /tienda.
   200|
   201|---
   202|
   203|### M5. Add structured data for products (Product schema)
   204|Only Store schema exists. Each product page should have Product schema with price, availability, image.
   205|
   206|**Files:** `components/pages/product-content.tsx` (already partially done — verify)
   207|
   208|---
   209|
   210|### M6. Add low-stock and "agotado" badges to product cards
   211|The tienda page already has stock badge code but it needs stock data populated in the DB.
   212|
   213|**Files:** `components/pages/tienda-content.tsx` lines 28-33
   214|
   215|---
   216|
   217|### M7. Add free-shipping threshold progress bar in cart
   218|Show how close the customer is to free shipping based on cart total vs thresholds in config.
   219|
   220|**Files:** `components/cart-sidebar.tsx`
   221|
   222|---
   223|
   224|### M8. Add invoice generation for Paraguayan SIFEN compliance
   225|Paraguay requires electronic invoicing (factura electrónica). This is needed before scaling.
   226|
   227|---
   228|
   229|### M9. Add order tracking portal for customers
   230|Customer-facing page at `/pedido/buscar` exists but needs real order lookup by ID/phone.
   231|
   232|**Files:** `components/order-tracking-form.tsx`
   233|
   234|---
   235|
   236|### M10. Add mobile bottom navigation bar
   237|Standard in LatAm e-commerce. Quick access to: Home, Tienda, Carrito, Cuenta, WhatsApp.
   238|
   239|---
   240|
   241|## 5. CHECKLIST SUMMARY
   242|
   243|| Priority | Item | Status |
   244||----------|------|--------|
   245|| CRITICAL | CartProvider in root layout (`app/layout.tsx`) | [ ] NOT DONE |
   246|| CRITICAL | Homepage stats show 0 (`content/es.json`) | [ ] NOT DONE |
   247|| CRITICAL | Real product photos (replace SVGs/illustrations) | [ ] NOT DONE |
   248|| CRITICAL | Connect payment gateway (Pagopar/Bancard keys) | [ ] NOT DONE |
   249|| CRITICAL | Fix /productos nav duplicate or misroute | [ ] NOT DONE |
   250|| HIGH | Admin dashboard stats querying wrong table | [ ] NOT DONE |
   251|| HIGH | Cart accessible site-wide | [ ] NOT DONE |
   252|| HIGH | Real phone/WhatsApp numbers in config | [ ] NOT DONE |
   253|| HIGH | Guest checkout / WhatsApp-only ordering | [ ] NOT DONE |
   254|| HIGH | GA4 configuration | [ ] NOT DONE |
   255|| MEDIUM | Google/Facebook OAuth setup | [ ] NOT DONE |
   256|| MEDIUM | WhatsApp CTA numbers | [ ] NOT DONE |
   257|| MEDIUM | Email sending (Resend API key) | [ ] NOT DONE |
   258|| MEDIUM | Breadcrumbs | [ ] NOT DONE |
   259|| MEDIUM | Product schema JSON-LD | [ ] PARTIALLY DONE |
   260|| MEDIUM | Low-stock / agotado badges | [ ] EXISTS IN CODE, NEEDS DATA |
   261|| MEDIUM | Free-shipping progress bar | [ ] NOT DONE |
   262|| MEDIUM | Invoice generation (SIFEN) | [ ] NOT DONE |
   263|| MEDIUM | Order tracking portal | [ ] NOT DONE |
   264|| MEDIUM | Mobile bottom nav bar | [ ] NOT DONE |
   265|
   266|---
   267|
   268|## QUICK WINS (can be done in <1 hour total)
   269|
   270|1. **Fix homepage stats** — edit `content/es.json` lines with real numbers (5 min)
   271|2. **Fix /productos nav** — remove duplicate link in `components/header.tsx` (2 min)

{"content":"# El Viajero - Complete Client Feedback & Requirements Tracker

**Client:** El Viajero (Tienda El Viajero)
**Contact:** WhatsApp [REDACTED_PHONE]
**Live URLs:** https://el-viajero.paragu-ai.com | https://viajero.paragu-ai.com
**Repo:** /root/elviajero (elviajero-comercio on GitHub)
**Stack:** Next.js 15, Tailwind 4, TypeScript, Supabase (ej_products, ej_orders, etc.)
**Maturity:** HIGH - 107 components, full e-commerce, admin panel

---

## LAST UPDATED: [REDACTED_PHONE]

---

## ✅ RESOLVED ISSUES

### 1. 🚨 Product Page 500 Error (FIXED [REDACTED_PHONE])
**Status:** ✅ RESOLVED
**Reported by:** User via error message + console logs
**Issue:** Product pages showing "Error del servidor" + React error #310 (infinite re-render loop)
**Root Cause:** `createClient()` called on every render, creating new Supabase reference. useEffect had `[supabase]` dependency → infinite loop
**Fix Applied:** Changed useEffect dependency from `[supabase]` to `[]` so it runs once on mount
**Files Modified:** `components/pages/product-content.tsx`
**Deployed:** Commit `1ab554e`, pushed to GitHub, GitHub Actions deploying

---

## ⚠️ CRITICAL ISSUES (Not Yet Addressed)

### C1. 🖼️ Image Performance - 133MB Unoptimized PNGs
**Source:** CRO Roast ([REDACTED_PHONE])
**Impact:** HIGH - Users on 3G/4G connections download 30+ MB just to load catalog = 25+ seconds white space, abandoned site
**Current State:** 82 PNG images, 0 WebP files, product images are 1.4–2.9 MB each
**Fix Required:**
- Convert all to WebP format
- Serve via `<picture>` with `srcset`
- Add `loading="lazy"`
- Target <100KB per product image
**Estimated Effort:** 4-6 hours

### C2. 🏗️ Dual Data Source Chaos
**Source:** CRO Roast ([REDACTED_PHONE])
**Impact:** MEDIUM - If Supabase is slow/down, products shown may not match actual inventory
**Current State:**
- Tries Supabase DB first, silently falls back to static JSON on error
- No error handling in fallback
- Prices stored as formatted strings ("Gs. 450.000") requiring regex parsing everywhere
**Fix Required:**
- Store prices as integers (cents/guaraníes)
- Parse once, format for display only
- Proper error handling for Supabase queries (display error to user)
**Estimated Effort:** 2-4 hours

### C3. 🛒 Checkout Problems
**Source:** CRO Roast ([REDACTED_PHONE])
**Impact:** HIGH - Customers can't complete purchase online
**Current State:**
- Hardcoded WhatsApp number (`[REDACTED_PHONE]`) in checkout page
- "WhatsApp/Transferencia" is DEFAULT payment - redirects user to WhatsApp instead of completing checkout
- Bancard/PagoPar listed but minimal implementation
- No form validation - user can submit empty order
**Fix Required:**
- Extract WhatsApp number to env/config (not hardcoded)
- Implement real payment gateway completion flow (no redirect to WhatsApp)
- Add form validation before proceeding to Step 3
**Estimated Effort:** 4-8 hours

### C4. 🔍 Search & Filters Bug
**Source:** CRO Roast ([REDACTED_PHONE])
**Impact:** MEDIUM - Brand filter literally doesn't work
**Current State:**
- Filter component has `setTimeout` callback hack (anti-pattern)
- `useMemo` missing `brandFilter` and `pricePreset` in dependency array
- Creates flash of unfiltered content, race conditions on rapid filter changes
**Fix Required:**
- Remove `setTimeout` callback, return from `useMemo` properly
- Add `brandFilter, pricePreset` to dependency array
**Estimated Effort:** 2-3 hours

### C5. 📱 SEO - No Metadata on Tienda/Product Pages
**Source:** CRO Roast ([REDACTED_PHONE])
**Impact:** HIGH - Invisible to Google Shopping, Product rich results, basic SERP visibility
**Current State:**
- Tienda page exports no metadata
- No `<title>`, no meta description, no Open Graph
- Product pages lack `generateMetadata()`
- Only SEO is in root layout (generic "El Viajero — Tu Aventura Empieza Acá")
**Fix Required:**
- Add `generateMetadata()` to tienda page with CollectionPage schema
- Add `generateMetadata()` to all product pages with Product schema (price, availability, image, offers)
**Estimated Effort:** 3-5 hours

### C6. 🧭 Navigation Confusion
**Source:** CRO Roast ([REDACTED_PHONE]) + Upgrade Plan
**Impact:** LOW - Confusing routing
**Current State:**
- `es.json` has TWO navigation arrays:
  - "items" with Productos → /productos
  - "ui" with Tienda → /tienda
- User sees "Tienda" in nav but breadcrumb leads nowhere useful
**Fix Required:**
- Remove duplicate /productos nav link or redirect properly
- Consolidate to single nav structure
**Estimated Effort:** 1 hour

### C7. 💰 Price String Parsing Hell
**Source:** CRO Roast ([REDACTED_PHONE])
**Impact:** MEDIUM - Brittle, locale-dependent, prone to breaking
**Current State:**
- Every price stored as "Gs. 450.000" string with thousand separators
- Requires `parseInt(s.replace(/[^\d]/g, ""))` everywhere
- Used in: filters, product cards, checkout, price comparisons
**Fix Required:**
- Store prices as integers in DB
- Parse once at display layer, format with proper locale
**Estimated Effort:** 2-3 hours

### C8. 🏛️ CartProvider Not in Root Layout
**Source:** Upgrade Plan ([REDACTED_PHONE])
**Impact:** HIGH - Cart crashes on non-wrapped pages
**Current State:**
- `CartProvider` only wrapped around `/tienda`, `/producto/[slug]`, `/checkout`, `/`
- `<CartBadge>` in header calls `useCart()` directly
- Pages like `/blog`, `/nosotros`, `/contacto`, `/faq`, `/admin/*`, `/mi-cuenta/*`, `/categoria/*` crash at runtime
**Fix Required:**
- Move `CartProvider` wrapping from individual page wrappers into `app/layout.tsx`
**Estimated Effort:** 30 minutes

### C9. 📊 Admin Dashboard Stats Query Wrong Table
**Source:** Upgrade Plan ([REDACTED_PHONE])
**Impact:** MEDIUM - Admin stats show 0
**Current State:**
- Admin dashboard queries `products` table instead of `ej_products`
- All pages should use `ej_products` (Supabase migrated schema)
**Fix Required:**
- Change query from `products` to `ej_products` in `/app/admin/page.tsx` line 7
**Estimated Effort:** 10 minutes

### C10. 🖼️ Product Images are Still Illustrations
**Source:** CRO Roast + Upgrade Plan
**Impact:** HIGH - Trust killer for customers
**Current State:**
- Products use generated illustration images (`/images/marketing/products/*.png`)
- No real product photos from client
- Placeholder SVGs instead of photos
**Fix Required:**
- Replace with real product photos
- OR enable admin image upload to Supabase storage bucket (`ej_product_images`)
- OR use high-quality Unsplash stock photos as temporary
**Estimated Effort:** Depends on client (4-8 hours)

### C11. 💳 No Payment Gateways Connected
**Source:** Upgrade Plan + STATUS.md
**Impact:** HIGH - Customers cannot pay online
**Current State:**
- Checkout API has code for Pagopar, Bancard, Stripe
- All three fall back to WhatsApp when API keys are missing
- `.env` file lacks real `PAGOPAR_PUBLIC_KEY`, `PAGOPAR_PRIVATE_KEY`, `BANCARD_PUBLIC_KEY`, `BANCARD_PRIVATE_KEY`, `STRIPE_SECRET_KEY`
**Fix Required:**
- Get real payment gateway API keys from Osmar
- Add to `.env.local` and `docker-compose.yml`
- Test end-to-end checkout flow
**Estimated Effort:** Depends on Osmar (2-4 hours)

### C12. 📞 Real Phone/WhatsApp Numbers Missing
**Source:** Upgrade Plan + STATUS.md
**Impact:** HIGH - Customers can't call the store
**Current State:**
- All CTAs use placeholder `[REDACTED_PHONE]`
- WhatsApp button uses hardcoded number
**Fix Required:**
- Update `config/site.json` and `content/es.json` with real numbers
**Estimated Effort:** 10 minutes

---

## 🔴 CRITICAL INFRASTRUCTURE ISSUES

### I1. CartProvider Scope Problem
**Status:** 🚨 URGENT
**Impact:** Cart functionality broken on all pages except /tienda, /producto, /checkout, /
**Fix:** Move CartProvider to `app/layout.tsx` (C8 above)

### I2. Supabase vs SQLite Dual Database
**Status:** 🚨 URGENT for production
**Current State:**
- Supabase migration completed (ej_products, ej_orders, etc.)
- But some code may still reference SQLite `data/viajero.db`
**Fix:**
- Audit all code paths for SQLite references
- Ensure all DB calls use Supabase client
**Estimated Effort:** 2-4 hours

---

## 🎯 FEATURE REQUESTS (From Build Wishlist)

### F1. Supabase Migration (Completed)
**Status:** ✅ DONE
**Details:** Full schema with RLS policies, 34 seeded products, 9 tables migrated

### F2. Real Payment Gateway
**Priority:** HIGH
**Details:** Bancard vPOS integration - complete checkout flow without WhatsApp redirect
**Estimated Effort:** 4-8 hours (requires API keys from Osmar)

### F3. Stock Sync System
**Priority:** MEDIUM
**Details:** Reconcile online store with physical shop inventory
**Estimated Effort:** 8-12 hours

### F4. Auto-order to WhatsApp
**Priority:** MEDIUM
**Details:** New orders → auto-forward to shop WhatsApp ([REDACTED_PHONE])
**Estimated Effort:** 4-6 hours

### F5. Delivery Tracking
**Priority:** MEDIUM
**Details:** Real-time courier integration
**Estimated Effort:** 6-10 hours

### F6. Product Variant Matrix
**Priority:** MEDIUM
**Details:** Size × color × material × price per variant
**Estimated Effort:** 8-12 hours

---

## ✅ WHAT'S WORKING

### Full E-commerce Flow
- Cart sidebar with add/remove/quantities/multi-select/merger
- Checkout with shipping zones, address form, Supabase order creation
- Payment gateway routing (Pagopar/Bancard/Stripe/WhatsApp)
- Orders with status tracking
- Shipping calculator (COD, pickup, delivery zones)
- Coupons (percentage/fixed), BOGO, bundle discounts
- Order confirmation with WhatsApp notification

### Admin Panel
- Full dashboard with sidebar navigation

- Orders management (view, status updates, export)
- Categories management
- Customers management
- Coupons/Promo codes management
- Reviews management
- Blog editor
- Theme editor
- Subscribers management
- Enrich AI features
- Stats dashboard

### SEO

- Open Graph tags
- Twitter cards

### UX Features

### Auth System

- Custom login/register pages
- Admin role check
- Session persistence

### Navigation
- Header with mobile hamburger
- Footer with all links
- Category icons
- Bottom mobile navigation

---

## 🎨 BRANDING & DESIGN ISSUES

### D1. Logo Problems
**Source:** Brutal Asset Critique
**Current:** 200x60 SVG, Poppins font (not on most systems), tiny 28px icon, generic mountain gradient
**Issues:**
- Icon too small for mobile
- Font fallback issues
- Generic outdoor store look (not camping/travel specific)
- Green-to-blue gradient wrong for brand
**Fix Required:** Complete redesign as outlined in upgrade plan
**Estimated Effort:** 1 hour

### D2. Favicon
**Current:** Green rounded rect with "EV" text, looks like generic app icon
**Fix Required:** Mountain peak + tent icon @64x64, must be recognizable at 16x16
**Estimated Effort:** 30 minutes

### D3. OG Image
**Current:** 1200x630 gradient, "EV" in white rounded box, emoji in OG images
**Fix Required:** Real brand photography or polished illustration with proper typography hierarchy
**Estimated Effort:** 1-2 hours

### D4. Category SVGs
**Current:** 400x267 SVGs with emoji text, 20% opacity backgrounds make photos barely visible
**Fix Required:** Replace with high-contrast photo-realistic backgrounds, darker tones for 20% opacity visibility
**Estimated Effort:** 2 hours

### D5. Category Hero Banners
**Current:** Photorealistic-style PNGs (1920x600), gradient sky + bokeh circles, no actual subjects
**Fix Required:** Real product photos or AI-generated images with actual subjects (tents, fishing rods, etc.)
**Estimated Effort:** 4-6 hours

### D6. PWA Icons
**Current:** Simple green rounded rect, generated programmatically, jagged edges, no anti-aliasing
**Fix Required:** Properly rendered SVG → PNG conversion at correct sizes, or dedicated PWA icon set
**Estimated Effort:** 1 hour

---
